title: Taiwan MND Detects 19 PLA Aircraft Sorties — Highest Single-Day Count of Late-March Spike
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 19 sorties of PLA aircraft, 9 PLAN vessels, and 2 official ships operating around Taiwan on March 29 — the highest single-day sortie count of the late-March escalation spike. 13 of 19 sorties crossed the Taiwan Strait median line, penetrating northern, central, southwestern, and eastern ADIZ sectors simultaneously, continuing the multi-sector pattern established on March 28 (15 sorties, 11 crossing). The three-day progression — 6 sorties (Mar 27) → 15 sorties (Mar 28) → 19 sorties (Mar 29) — represents the sharpest week-over-week surge since the March 25 spike of 16 sorties (13 crossing). PLA Eastern Theater Command again described activity as 'air-sea joint training.' ROC Armed Forces deployed CAP aircraft, Navy ships, and coastal missile systems. Analysts at AEI/ISW note the sustained multi-sector simultaneous crossing pattern is qualitatively more significant than mere aircraft counts, as it forces Taiwan's air defense to respond across all sectors simultaneously — a stress-test characteristic of rehearsal for operational conditions.

title: AIT Director Greene Publicly Backs 5% GDP Defense Target and NT$1.25T Special Budget
detail: American Institute in Taiwan (AIT) Director Raymond Greene stated in a March 29 TVBS interview that the Trump administration is asking all partners including Taiwan to raise defense spending to 5% of GDP, and that Washington supports President Lai Ching-te's goal of reaching that target by 2030. Greene publicly endorsed Taiwan's NT$1.25 trillion (~US$40 billion) 8-year special defense budget and called on all parties — including the opposition KMT and TPP — to pass it, citing the urgency of the military threat environment. The statement is the most direct US public endorsement of the full NT$1.25T bill (vs. the KMT's NT$380B and TPP's NT$400B alternatives), coming on the same day as Taiwan's highest PLA aircraft sortie count of the late-March spike (19 sorties). Greene's TVBS appearance represents a public escalation of US pressure on Taiwan's legislature, following US senators' visits to Taipei on March 28 with the same message. Taiwan's MND has warned that failure to pass the special budget will delay US weapons procurement through end of 2026.

title: Taiwan Army Conducts Combat Readiness Drills on Matsu; Missile Output Expansion Announced
detail: The Taiwan Army conducted combat readiness drills on the Matsu islands on March 29, in the context of elevated PLA activity in the adjacent sea lanes. Simultaneously, Taiwan announced plans to expand missile output to strengthen its T-Dome air defense system — a domestic successor to the Patriot family — as the special defense budget deadlock has delayed procurement of some US-made systems. The Matsu island chain, located just 11 km off Fuzhou, China, has been subject to increasing PLA drone overflights and coast guard pressure since 2022. The exercises come amid calls by AIT Director Greene for Taiwan to pass the special defense budget that covers both the T-Dome program and US-origin HIMARS, PAC-3, and NASAMS systems. Taiwan also confirmed that the HIMARS Letter of Offer and Acceptance signed before the March 26 deadline secured the delivery queue position.
